segments.text: And when they came nigh to Jerusalem, unto Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount of Olives, he sendeth forth two of his disciples
And saith unto them
And they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door without in a place where two ways met; and they loose him
What do ye, loosing the colt?
segments.speaker: Certain people standing there

segments.text: And they said unto them even as Jesus had commanded: and they let them go
And they brought the colt to Jesus, and cast their garments on him; and he sat upon him
And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut down branches off the trees, and strawed them in the way
Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Blessed be the kingdom of our father David, that cometh in the name of the Lord: Hosanna in the highest
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: And Jesus entered into Jerusalem, and into the temple: and when he had looked round about upon all things, and now the eventide was come, he went out unto Bethany with the twelve
And on the morrow, when they were come from Bethany, he was hungry
And seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came, if haply he might find any thing thereon: and when he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for the time of figs was not yet
And his disciples heard it
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And they come to Jerusalem: and Jesus went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves
And would not suffer that any man should carry any vessel through the temple
And he taught, saying unto them
And the scribes and chief priests heard it, and sought how they might destroy him: for they feared him, because all the people was astonished at his doctrine
And when even was come, he went out of the city
And in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots
Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And Jesus answering saith unto them
And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders
By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority to do these things?
segments.speaker: Chief priests, scribes, and elders

segments.text: And Jesus answered and said unto them
If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then did ye not believe him?
segments.speaker: Chief priests, scribes, and elders

segments.text: But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all men counted John, that he was a prophet indeed
We cannot tell
segments.speaker: Chief priests, scribes, and elders

segments.text: And Jesus answering saith unto them
